title: 1. Basic Usage
text: Three small synthetic polygons, built base sf calls, cover useful range boundary behaviour: star (deep, evenly-spaced notches), spiral (single winding corridor, notches ), blob hole (irregular ring interior void).
code: make_star <- function(n_points, r_outer = 1, r_inner = 0.5, center = c(0, 0)) {   n <- n_points * 2   angles <- seq(pi / 2, pi / 2 + 2 * pi, length.out = n + 1)[1:n]   radii  <- rep(c(r_outer, r_inner), n_points)   x <- center[1] + radii * cos(angles)   y <- center[2] + radii * sin(angles)   coords <- rbind(cbind(x, y), c(x[1], y[1]))   st_polygon(list(coords)) }  make_turtle_path <- function(n_steps, angle_deg, step0 = 1, step_growth = 0) {   angle <- 0   pos   <- c(0, 0)   pts   <- matrix(pos, ncol = 2)   step  <- step0   for (i in seq_len(n_steps)) {     angle <- angle + angle_deg * pi / 180     pos   <- pos + step * c(cos(angle), sin(angle))     pts   <- rbind(pts, pos)     step  <- step + step_growth   }   st_linestring(pts) }  make_spiral <- function(n_steps = 48, angle_deg = 24, step0 = 0.35,                          step_growth = 0.09, width = 0.35) {   st_buffer(make_turtle_path(n_steps, angle_deg, step0, step_growth),             dist = width, endCapStyle = "FLAT", joinStyle = "MITRE", mitreLimit = 3) }  make_blob_hole <- function(n = 14, seed = 1, roughness = 0.55, hole_frac = 0.3) {   set.seed(seed)   angles <- sort(runif(n, 0, 2 * pi))   radii  <- 1 + roughness * (runif(n) - 0.5) * 2   outer  <- cbind(radii * cos(angles), radii * sin(angles))   outer  <- rbind(outer, outer[1, ])    # the hole is the SAME irregular outline scaled down by sqrt(hole_frac)   # (so hole area / outer area ~= hole_frac) and traced in reverse, so sf   # treats it as an interior ring rather than a second shell. Scaling every   # radius by the same constant keeps the hole strictly inside the outer   # boundary at every angle, so the result is always a valid polygon.   hole <- outer[nrow(outer):1, ] * sqrt(hole_frac)    st_polygon(list(outer, hole)) }  star      <- make_star(6, r_outer = 1, r_inner = 0.4) spiral    <- make_spiral() blob_hole <- make_blob_hole()  basic_shapes <- list(star = star, spiral = spiral, "blob with hole" = blob_hole)  # geom_sf() + facet_wrap() can't use free scales, so normalise each shape # onto a common bounding box (center it, scale to unit span) before faceting normalize_geom <- function(geom, center, scale) (geom - center) * scale shapes_norm <- lapply(names(basic_shapes), function(nm) {   g      <- st_sfc(basic_shapes[[nm]])   bb     <- st_bbox(g)   center <- unname(c((bb["xmin"] + bb["xmax"]) / 2, (bb["ymin"] + bb["ymax"]) / 2))   span   <- max(bb["xmax"] - bb["xmin"], bb["ymax"] - bb["ymin"])   st_sf(shape = nm, geometry = normalize_geom(g, center, 1 / span)) }) shapes_sf <- do.call(rbind, shapes_norm)  ggplot(shapes_sf) +   geom_sf(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, color = "grey20") +   facet_wrap(~ shape) +   theme_void(base_size = 11) +   theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

title: 1. Basic Usage
text: index function - convexity_index(), moment_of_inertia_index(), moment_isotropy_index(), directional_balance_index(), span_index(), radial_concentration_index(), depth_index() (mesh-based: needs CDT triangulation polygon), plus hull_ratio_index(), polsby_popper_index(), width_length_ratio_index(), reock_index(), detour_index(), exchange_index() (classic redistricting-literature metrics: needs polygon’s boundary, convex hull, minimum bounding circle, triangulation ) - returns list index plus supporting detail (triangle mesh used, evaluated lines, etc.). Called directly shape, example,: Running seven mesh-based indices separately re-triangulates polygon seven times. shape_indices() triangulates returns thirteen named vector, might implications speed. spiral scores low every index (long, narrow, winding corridor far convex far disk-like); blob’s hole drags indices relative solid blob outer boundary; star sits , close hull_ratio_index twin lower convexity_index, - unlike hull_ratio_index - sensitive evenly-spaced notches cut . polsby_popper_index particular penalises blob’s hole twice , : area already net hole (every index measures net area), perimeter grows , since st_boundary() returns every ring - hole’s boundary, just outer one - st_length() sums across . shape hole cut genuinely less compact solid version, terms moving direction right behaviour - ’s universal convention. Polsby-Popper implementations redistricting literature measure outer ring’s length, treating interior holes (small enclave, data-digitisation artifact) compactness-irrelevant. polsby_popper_index() ignore_holes argument switch two conventions, deliberately - make function silently answer two different questions depending flag. need match outer-ring-definition, strip interior rings input first: st_polygon(your_geometry[1]) keeps first (outer) ring single polygon.

title: 1. Basic Usage
text: convexity_index(), span_index(), radial_concentration_index(), directional_balance_index(), depth_index() deterministic argument.moment_of_inertia_index(), moment_isotropy_index(), six classic metrics don’t argument. always computed directly CDT mesh / convex hull / bounding circle, unaffected choice. deterministic = TRUE (default five) computes index fixed grid CDT mesh - O(n^2) piece count convexity/span, O(n) radial_concentration/directional_balance/depth triangle’s subdivision depth (256 points) scaled area, mesh’s largest triangles pay full cost. ’s called deterministic, exact, deliberately: shapes small concavities relative triangle size, (radial_concentration/directional_balance/depth) integral closed form , ’s still approximation true value, just non-random one (see vignette("b-understanding-convexity-index")’s convexity section ). deterministic = FALSE instead computes Monte Carlo estimate: five functions draw n_lines independent random samples average property : - convexity_index() fraction connecting line’s length falls outside polygon, - span_index() line’s length - radial_concentration_index() distance sampled point shape’s geometric median - directional_balance_index() bearing sampled point shape’s centroid, - depth_index() distance sampled point shape’s boundary. convexity_index() ever actually builds line geometry. Sharing argument name means one n_lines sample count five functions. Monte Carlo estimate noisy, close. relative deterministic one, noise shrinks n_lines grows.
code: det_compare <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(basic_shapes), function(nm) {   g <- basic_shapes[[nm]]   data.frame(     shape              = nm,     ci_deterministic   = convexity_index(g, deterministic = TRUE)$index,     ci_random_line     = convexity_index(g, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 3000, seed = 1)$index,     span_deterministic = span_index(g, deterministic = TRUE)$index,     span_random_pair   = span_index(g, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 3000, seed = 1)$index,     rci_deterministic  = radial_concentration_index(g, deterministic = TRUE)$index,     rci_random_point   = radial_concentration_index(g, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 3000, seed = 1)$index,     db_deterministic   = directional_balance_index(g, deterministic = TRUE)$index,     db_random_point    = directional_balance_index(g, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 3000, seed = 1)$index,     depth_deterministic = depth_index(g, deterministic = TRUE)$index,     depth_random_point   = depth_index(g, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 3000, seed = 1)$index,     moi                = moment_of_inertia_index(g)$index,     moment_isotropy    = moment_isotropy_index(g)$index,     hull_ratio         = hull_ratio_index(g)$index   ) })) knitr::kable(format = "html", det_compare, digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)

what: Standalone utilities: coarsening and refining the mesh
title: 1. Basic Usage
text: convexity_index() always works polygon’s CDT triangle mesh - n_quad controls quadrature refinement mesh (see ), ’s alternative tessellation choose. Separately, two standalone mesh utilities available downstream geometry work, opposite directions - neither wired thirteen indices. convex_decompose() gives Hertel-Mehlhorn convex decomposition (fewer, larger convex pieces raw triangle mesh): subdivide_mesh() goes way - , smaller triangles, via area-adaptive medial subdivision radial_concentration_index() uses internally quadrature (see vignette("e-understanding-radial-concentration-index")), just kept triangle geometries rather collapsed weighted centroids:
code: tri    <- cdt_triangles(star) pieces <- convex_decompose(star) nrow(tri)     # many small triangles [1] 10 nrow(pieces)  # fewer, larger convex pieces [1] 7 fine <- subdivide_mesh(star) nrow(fine)    # more, smaller triangles than tri [1] 2560

what: Applying it to an sf object, row by row
title: 1. Basic Usage
text: Real polygons usually arrive rows sf data frame. shape_indices_sf() byrow = TRUE (default) runs shape_indices() every row independently appends four columns. Two North Carolina counties sf package - Wake (simple, nearly-convex Piedmont county) Dare (mainland piece plus separated barrier-island strip) - show range:
code: nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE) %>%   st_transform(32119)   # NC state plane (meters) - nc.shp ships in NAD27 lon/lat  pair     <- nc[nc$NAME %in% c("Wake", "Dare"), ] pair_res <- shape_indices_sf(pair)  pair_res %>%   st_drop_geometry() %>%   select(NAME, convexity_index, moment_of_inertia_index, span_index, hull_ratio_index) %>%   knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 3, row.names = FALSE) ggplot(pair_res) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = convexity_index), color = "grey20") +   scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0, 1)) +   labs(fill = "CI")

code: cell <- function(cx, cy) st_polygon(list(rbind(   c(cx - 0.5, cy - 0.5), c(cx + 0.5, cy - 0.5),   c(cx + 0.5, cy + 0.5), c(cx - 0.5, cy + 0.5), c(cx - 0.5, cy - 0.5)))) ctr <- expand.grid(x = -1:1, y = -1:1) grid9 <- st_sf(pop = ifelse(ctr$x == 0 & ctr$y == 0, 0, 10),                geometry = st_sfc(mapply(cell, ctr$x, ctr$y, SIMPLIFY = FALSE), crs = 3857))  solid_block <- shape_indices_sf(grid9, byrow = FALSE, id = "solid_block")            # keeps all 9 ring        <- shape_indices_sf(grid9, byrow = FALSE, weights = "pop", id = "ring")  # centre excluded  rbind(solid_block, ring) %>%   st_drop_geometry() %>%   knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 3, row.names = FALSE) grid_compare <- rbind(   solid_block %>% mutate(label = "solid_block (weights = NULL)"),   ring        %>% mutate(label = "ring (centre cell weight = 0)") )  ggplot(grid_compare) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = label), alpha = 0.6, color = "grey20", show.legend = FALSE) +   facet_wrap(~ label) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("solid_block (weights = NULL)" = "steelblue",                                 "ring (centre cell weight = 0)" = "firebrick")) +   theme_void(base_size = 11) +   theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

Simplifying row independently first - natural-looking alternative, wrong one - simplifies shared edge differently either side , turning used clean tessellation thousands sliver gaps rows longer quite touch. concrete example, one real 50,424-row Census-block dataset: unioning raw rows gives one clean, ~50,000-vertex polygon, independently simplifying every row first unioning gives 1,851 disjoint sliver fragments instead - total boundary simplifying , scale difference computation fits memory one doesn’t. code: library(furrr)  future::plan(future::sequential) t_seq_rows <- system.time(   res_seq <- shape_indices_sf(nc, deterministic_max_tri = 60, n_lines = 6000, seed = 1,                                parallel_rows = FALSE) )  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4) t_par_rows <- system.time(   res_par_rows <- shape_indices_sf(nc, deterministic_max_tri = 60, n_lines = 6000, seed = 1,                                     parallel_rows = TRUE) ) future::plan(future::sequential)  timing <- data.frame(   mode    = c("parallel_rows = FALSE", "parallel_rows = TRUE (4 workers)"),   elapsed = c(t_seq_rows[["elapsed"]], t_par_rows[["elapsed"]]) ) timing$speedup <- timing$elapsed[1] / timing$elapsed knitr::kable(format = "html", timing, digits = 2, row.names = FALSE) ggplot(timing, aes(x = mode, y = elapsed)) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue", width = 0.6) +   labs(x = NULL, y = "elapsed, seconds") +   coord_flip()

title: 2. Understanding Convexity Index
text: tutorial covers shape index called convexity_index (CI). polygon convex line two interior points never crosses boundary. CI turns continuous measure: fraction lines stay entirely inside? practice: build constrained Delaunay triangulation (CDT), draw line every pair triangle representative points, take area-weighted fraction stays inside. score 1 means every line stayed inside; single convex blob. sections show index gets wrong compare indices literature. construction - probability random pair interior points stays connected straight line - matches convexity measure introduced Zunic & Rosin (2004).1 Two comparison indices recur throughout tutorial: moment_of_inertia_index (roundness, convexity) hull_ratio_index (simpler, boundary-sensitive convexity proxy) - introduced full . rest tutorial works shape features affect CI: boundary complexity, overall shape, size, symmetry, holes, multi-part polygons, raster resolution. properties matter choosing shape indicator holds real data.

title: 2. Understanding Convexity Index
text: “Delaunay triangulation” converts set points well-shaped triangles, using empty-circumcircle property avoid long, sliver-shaped ones. Applied naively polygon’s vertices, happily fill triangles across holes gaps, since sees points, edges. constrained Delaunay triangulation (CDT) instead treats polygon’s edges hole boundaries constraints every triangle must respect. triangulation covers exactly polygon’s true interior, shown .

code: CI_DETERMINISTIC_MAX_TRI <- 25  #' Dispatcher for every CI number below: deterministic = TRUE #' (convexity_index()'s own n_quad = 3 default applies automatically) for #' meshes with CI_DETERMINISTIC_MAX_TRI triangles or fewer, #' deterministic = FALSE (5000 lines) above that. Adds `deterministic_used` #' so callers can flag approximated values or draw the right kind of line #' in a diagnostic plot. ci_auto <- function(poly, prep = NULL) {   if (is.null(prep)) prep <- prepare_polygon(poly)   n_tri <- if (is.null(prep$tri)) 0 else nrow(prep$tri)   deterministic <- n_tri <= CI_DETERMINISTIC_MAX_TRI   res <- suppressWarnings(convexity_index(poly, deterministic = deterministic,                                            n_lines = 5000, prep = prep))   res$deterministic_used <- deterministic   res }

code: star <- st_sfc(make_star(6, 5, 2)) prep <- prepare_polygon(star) tri  <- prep$tri d    <- as.numeric(st_distance(st_centroid(st_geometry(tri)), st_centroid(star))) w_centre <- exp(-d / mean(d)) w_edge   <- exp( d / mean(d))  data.frame(   weighting = c(weight_thumb(tri, rep(1, nrow(tri))), weight_thumb(tri, w_centre), weight_thumb(tri, w_edge)),   name = c("uniform (area)", "toward centre", "toward edge"),   moment_of_inertia = c(     moment_of_inertia_index(star, prep = prep, weight = tri$area)$index,     moment_of_inertia_index(star, prep = prep, weight = w_centre)$index,     moment_of_inertia_index(star, prep = prep, weight = w_edge)$index) ) |> knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 3, row.names = FALSE, escape = FALSE) tbl_spokes <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(spokes), function(nm) {   poly <- st_sfc(spokes[[nm]])   data.frame(shape = shape_thumb(spokes[[nm]]), name = nm,              moment_of_inertia = moment_of_inertia_index(poly)$index,              convexity = suppressWarnings(convexity_index(poly, deterministic = FALSE,                                                             n_lines = 5000, seed = 1)$index)) })) knitr::kable(format = "html", tbl_spokes, digits = 4, row.names = FALSE, escape = FALSE) make_spokes_at <- function(r_in, r_out, angles, half_w) {   polys <- lapply(angles, function(a0) {     th <- seq(a0 - half_w, a0 + half_w, length.out = 12)     outer_pts <- cbind(r_out * cos(th), r_out * sin(th))     inner_pts <- cbind(r_in * cos(rev(th)), r_in * sin(rev(th)))     st_polygon(list(rbind(outer_pts, inner_pts, outer_pts[1, ])))   })   Reduce(function(p, q) st_union(st_sfc(p), st_sfc(q))[[1]], polys) } half_w <- 0.5 * pi / 4 evenly  <- st_sfc(make_spokes_at(3, 5, seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 5)[1:4], half_w)) bunched <- st_sfc(make_spokes_at(3, 5, c(0, 1.3, 2.7, 4.6), half_w))  # same 4 wedges, uneven bearings  moi_evenly  <- moment_of_inertia_index(evenly)$index moi_bunched <- moment_of_inertia_index(bunched)$index

code: subdivide <- function(v) {   m_ab <- (v[1,]+v[2,])/2; m_bc <- (v[2,]+v[3,])/2; m_ca <- (v[3,]+v[1,])/2   list(rbind(v[1,],m_ab,m_ca), rbind(v[2,],m_bc,m_ab), rbind(v[3,],m_ca,m_bc), rbind(m_ab,m_bc,m_ca)) } centroid_self_D <- function(v, k) {   tris <- list(v)   for (i in seq_len(k)) tris <- unlist(lapply(tris, subdivide), recursive = FALSE)   cen <- t(vapply(tris, colMeans, numeric(2)))   mean(as.matrix(dist(cen))) } v <- rbind(c(0,0), c(4,0), c(0,3))   # an arbitrary right triangle, legs 4 and 3 set.seed(1) n_mc <- 300000 r1 <- runif(n_mc); r2 <- runif(n_mc); flip <- (r1+r2) > 1 r1[flip] <- 1-r1[flip]; r2[flip] <- 1-r2[flip] P1 <- cbind(v[1,1]+r1*(v[2,1]-v[1,1])+r2*(v[3,1]-v[1,1]), v[1,2]+r1*(v[2,2]-v[1,2])+r2*(v[3,2]-v[1,2])) r1 <- runif(n_mc); r2 <- runif(n_mc); flip <- (r1+r2) > 1 r1[flip] <- 1-r1[flip]; r2[flip] <- 1-r2[flip] P2 <- cbind(v[1,1]+r1*(v[2,1]-v[1,1])+r2*(v[3,1]-v[1,1]), v[1,2]+r1*(v[2,2]-v[1,2])+r2*(v[3,2]-v[1,2])) D_true <- mean(sqrt(rowSums((P1-P2)^2)))  convergence <- data.frame(   depth = 0:6,   n_centroids = 4^(0:6),   D = vapply(0:6, function(k) centroid_self_D(v, k), numeric(1)) ) convergence$rel_error_pct <- 100 * (convergence$D - D_true) / D_true knitr::kable(format = "html", convergence, digits = c(0, 0, 4, 3),              col.names = c("subdivision depth", "centroids", "D estimate", "rel. error (%)"))

code: subdivide <- function(v) {   m_ab <- (v[1,]+v[2,])/2; m_bc <- (v[2,]+v[3,])/2; m_ca <- (v[3,]+v[1,])/2   list(rbind(v[1,],m_ab,m_ca), rbind(v[2,],m_bc,m_ab), rbind(v[3,],m_ca,m_bc), rbind(m_ab,m_bc,m_ca)) } centroid_self_D <- function(v, k) {   tris <- list(v)   for (i in seq_len(k)) tris <- unlist(lapply(tris, subdivide), recursive = FALSE)   cen <- t(vapply(tris, colMeans, numeric(2)))   mean(as.matrix(dist(cen))) } v <- rbind(c(0,0), c(4,0), c(0,3))   # an arbitrary right triangle, legs 4 and 3 set.seed(1) n_mc <- 300000 r1 <- runif(n_mc); r2 <- runif(n_mc); flip <- (r1+r2) > 1 r1[flip] <- 1-r1[flip]; r2[flip] <- 1-r2[flip] P1 <- cbind(v[1,1]+r1*(v[2,1]-v[1,1])+r2*(v[3,1]-v[1,1]), v[1,2]+r1*(v[2,2]-v[1,2])+r2*(v[3,2]-v[1,2])) r1 <- runif(n_mc); r2 <- runif(n_mc); flip <- (r1+r2) > 1 r1[flip] <- 1-r1[flip]; r2[flip] <- 1-r2[flip] P2 <- cbind(v[1,1]+r1*(v[2,1]-v[1,1])+r2*(v[3,1]-v[1,1]), v[1,2]+r1*(v[2,2]-v[1,2])+r2*(v[3,2]-v[1,2])) D_true <- mean(sqrt(rowSums((P1-P2)^2)))  convergence <- data.frame(   depth = 0:6,   n_centroids = 4^(0:6),   D = vapply(0:6, function(k) centroid_self_D(v, k), numeric(1)) ) convergence$rel_error_pct <- 100 * (convergence$D - D_true) / D_true knitr::kable(format = "html", convergence, digits = c(0, 0, 4, 3),              col.names = c("subdivision depth", "centroids", "D estimate", "rel. error (%)"))

code: # a fan triangulation from every vertex of a convex, irregular heptagon - # seven genuinely different triangulations of the exact same region ang <- c(10, 50, 100, 160, 210, 270, 320) * pi / 180 r   <- c(5, 4.2, 5.5, 4.8, 5.1, 4.5, 5.3) v   <- cbind(r * cos(ang), r * sin(ang)) heptagon <- st_polygon(list(rbind(v, v[1, ]))) n <- nrow(v)  fan_from <- function(apex) {   ord  <- ((apex + 0:(n - 2)) %% n) + 1   # the other n-1 vertices, in cyclic order   tris <- lapply(seq_len(n - 2), function(k) {     st_polygon(list(rbind(v[apex, ], v[ord[k], ], v[ord[k + 1], ], v[apex, ])))   })   st_sf(area = vapply(tris, function(t) as.numeric(st_area(st_sfc(t))), numeric(1)),         geometry = st_sfc(tris)) }  idx_cdt <- moment_isotropy_index(st_sfc(heptagon),                                   prep = list(poly = heptagon, tri = cdt_triangles(st_sfc(heptagon))))$index idx_fan <- vapply(1:n, function(apex) {   moment_isotropy_index(st_sfc(heptagon), prep = list(poly = heptagon, tri = fan_from(apex)))$index }, numeric(1))  data.frame(triangulation = c("CDT", paste("fan from vertex", 1:n)),            index = c(idx_cdt, idx_fan)) |> knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 10, row.names = FALSE)

code: prep <- prepare_polygon(st_sfc(disk)) tri <- prep$tri ang <- atan2(st_coordinates(st_centroid(st_geometry(tri)))[, 2],              st_coordinates(st_centroid(st_geometry(tri)))[, 1]) w_ew <- 1 + 3 * cos(ang)^2   # weight concentrated along the east-west axis  plain <- moment_isotropy_index(st_sfc(disk), prep = prep) weighted <- moment_isotropy_index(st_sfc(disk), prep = prep, weight = w_ew)  # the shape itself never changes here, so the thumbnail shows the WEIGHT # distribution (shaded by density) rather than a fixed outline - what # varies row to row is where the mass sits, not the boundary data.frame(   weighting = c(weight_thumb(tri, rep(1, nrow(tri))), weight_thumb(tri, w_ew)),   name = c("uniform (area)", "concentrated east-west"),   isotropy = c(plain$index, weighted$index) ) |> knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 3, row.names = FALSE, escape = FALSE)

title: 7. Understanding Directional Balance Index

Concretely: RR magnitude mean resultant vector eiθ(x)e^{\theta(x)}, θ(x)\theta(x) angle point xx seen shape’s mass centroid GG - index 1−R1 - R, [0,1][0, 1]. index uses - deliberately ignores. Unlike moment_of_inertia_index() moment_isotropy_index(), integrate position squared, index uses bearing θ(s)\theta(s), never distance. Distance carry information anyway: GG mass centroid definition, raw first moment position, ∫Pρ(s)(s−G)ds\int_P \rho(s)(s - G)\,ds, identically zero every shape. eiθ(s)e^{\theta(s)} discards distance keeps direction, precisely makes quantity informative - asks whether shape’s area, bearing bearing, spread evenly around centroid piled toward directions. One reading guard : balanced mean uniform. code: notchy <- st_sfc(make_star(6, 5.64, 0.6), crs = 3857) prep   <- prepare_polygon(notchy)  # naive: one point (the bare centroid) per triangle, no subdivision at all bnd <- st_boundary(prep$poly) tc  <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(st_geometry(prep$tri)))[, 1:2] d_naive <- as.numeric(st_distance(st_cast(st_sfc(st_multipoint(tc), crs = st_crs(prep$poly)), "POINT"), bnd)) mean_naive <- sum(prep$tri$area * d_naive) / sum(prep$tri$area)  res_adaptive <- depth_index(notchy, prep = prep) res_hires    <- depth_index(notchy, prep = prep, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 200000, seed = 1)  data.frame(   method = c("naive (bare centroid per triangle)", "adaptive subdivision (the package default)",              "Monte Carlo, n = 200,000 (ground truth)"),   mean_depth = c(mean_naive, res_adaptive$mean_depth, res_hires$mean_depth) ) |> knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 4) star8 <- st_sfc(make_star(8, 1, 0.4), crs = 3857) depth_index(star8)$index [1] 0.4479874 depth_index(star8, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 8000, seed = 1)$index [1] 0.4504543 disk <- st_sfc(make_disk(5, n = 60), crs = 3857) depth_index(disk)$index                                          # deterministic [1] 1.001851 depth_index(disk, deterministic = FALSE, n_lines = 5000, seed = 1)$index  # Monte Carlo [1] 1.007527

code: make_square <- function(half = 5) st_polygon(list(rbind(   c(-half, -half), c(half, -half), c(half, half), c(-half, half), c(-half, -half)))) make_rectangle <- function(w, h) st_polygon(list(rbind(   c(0, 0), c(w, 0), c(w, h), c(0, h), c(0, 0)))) make_disk <- function(r = 5, n = 60) st_buffer(st_sfc(st_point(c(0, 0))), dist = r, nQuadSegs = n)[[1]] make_star <- function(n_points, r_outer, r_inner) {   n <- n_points * 2   angles <- seq(pi / 2, pi / 2 + 2 * pi, length.out = n + 1)[1:n]   radii  <- rep(c(r_outer, r_inner), n_points)   x <- radii * cos(angles); y <- radii * sin(angles)   st_polygon(list(rbind(cbind(x, y), c(x[1], y[1])))) }  canonical <- list(   square         = make_square(5),   disk           = make_disk(5.64),   # same area as the square, for a fair side-by-side   `rect 2:1`     = make_rectangle(14.14, 7.07),   `rect 10:1`    = make_rectangle(31.6, 3.16),   `hexagon`      = st_polygon(list(rbind(t(sapply(seq(0, 300, 60) * pi / 180,                      function(a) c(5.5 * cos(a), 5.5 * sin(a)))), c(5.5, 0)))),   `star (mild)`  = make_star(6, 5.64, 3.5),   `star (sharp)` = make_star(6, 5.64, 0.6) )

code: make_square_with_hole <- function(outer_half = 5, hole_frac = 0.3) {   outer <- rbind(c(-outer_half, -outer_half), c(outer_half, -outer_half),                  c(outer_half, outer_half), c(-outer_half, outer_half), c(-outer_half, -outer_half))   hh <- outer_half * sqrt(hole_frac)   hole <- rbind(c(-hh, -hh), c(-hh, hh), c(hh, hh), c(hh, -hh), c(-hh, -hh))   st_polygon(list(outer, hole)) } make_dumbbell_gap <- function(gap) {   sq1 <- st_polygon(list(rbind(c(0, 0), c(2, 0), c(2, 2), c(0, 2), c(0, 0))))   sq2 <- st_polygon(list(rbind(c(2 + gap, 0), c(4 + gap, 0), c(4 + gap, 2), c(2 + gap, 2), c(2 + gap, 0))))   st_union(st_sfc(sq1, sq2)) } sq1 <- st_polygon(list(rbind(c(0, 0), c(2, 0), c(2, 2), c(0, 2), c(0, 0)))) sq2 <- st_polygon(list(rbind(c(2, 0), c(4, 0), c(4, 2), c(2, 2), c(2, 0)))) x <- st_sf(name = c("a", "b"), geometry = st_sfc(sq1, sq2, crs = 3857))  equal_weights   <- suppressWarnings(shape_indices_sf(x, byrow = FALSE, weights = c(1, 1),                                                        which = c("hull_ratio", "detour", "exchange"), id = "z")) unequal_weights <- suppressWarnings(shape_indices_sf(x, byrow = FALSE, weights = c(1, 1000),                                                        which = c("hull_ratio", "detour", "exchange"), id = "z")) zeroed_weight   <- suppressWarnings(shape_indices_sf(x, byrow = FALSE, weights = c(1, 0),                                                        which = c("hull_ratio", "detour", "exchange"), id = "z"))  data.frame(   case = c("weights = c(1, 1)", "weights = c(1, 1000)", "weights = c(1, 0)"),   rbind(st_drop_geometry(equal_weights)[, c("hull_ratio_index", "detour_index", "exchange_index")],         st_drop_geometry(unequal_weights)[, c("hull_ratio_index", "detour_index", "exchange_index")],         st_drop_geometry(zeroed_weight)[, c("hull_ratio_index", "detour_index", "exchange_index")]) ) |> knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 4, row.names = FALSE)

title: Moment isotropy index of a (multi)polygon's mass distribution — moment_isotropy_index
text: Ratio smaller larger principal moment mass inertia tensor, (0, 1]. 1 means mass distribution rotationally isotropic centroid (disk qualifies, shape 3-fold higher rotational symmetry - claim convexity_index()'s moment_of_inertia_index()'s "= 1 iff disk"). Lower values mean mass anisotropic - elongated along one direction - regardless whether shape convex dispersed. See vignette("f-understanding-moment-isotropy-index") derivation, bound proof, relates (differs ) classical eccentricity.

title: Validate and triangulate a polygon once — prepare_polygon
text: poly single sfg/sfc (MULTI)POLYGON simplify_tolerance optional st_simplify() tolerance. NULL (default) skips simplification entirely. Applied cleaning, triangulating - trades small, bounded amount boundary detail smaller mesh (fewer CDT triangles fewer total boundary edges, reduce every downstream index's cost memory use). Always geometry's CRS unit point simplification runs, necessarily metres: geographic (lon/lat) input gets auto-projected metric CRS first (always metres), input already projected CRS keeps whatever linear unit CRS uses - many US State Plane CRSs US survey feet, example. Warns unit metres, since silently-wrong unit makes tolerance value mean different amount simplification intended.

title: Radial concentration index: mean distance to the geometric median vs an equal-area circle — radial_concentration_index
text: poly single sfg/sfc (MULTI)POLYGON deterministic TRUE (default), compute fixed depth-4-subdivision point cloud (4^4 = 256 points per CDT triangle) - O(n) triangle count, large constant factor 256x expansion. FALSE, Monte Carlo n_lines points sampled directly weighted density instead - decouples cost mesh size, useful mesh large enough 256x subdivision cloud becomes bottleneck. n_lines number random points sample deterministic = FALSE (default 3000), function(n_tri) - argument name convexity_index()'s/span_index()'s Monte Carlo mode, one value passed shape_indices() sets every mesh index's sample count , even though draws single points, lines point-pairs. seed optional RNG seed, used deterministic = FALSE. prep optional pre-computed list(poly, tri) prepare_polygon(), skip re-triangulating also calling indices weight optional numeric vector, one entry per triangle prep$tri, substituting triangle's area/mass throughout. NULL (default) compares circle; otherwise reference becomes concentric annulus matching weight's histogram. points optional pre-drawn Nx2 coordinate matrix, meaningful deterministic = FALSE - primarily shape_indices()'s internal point-sharing mechanism (draw one sample reuse across convexity/span/radial_concentration instead drawing independently), typically supplied directly. simplify_tolerance passed prepare_polygon() prep NULL - see doc. Ignored (error) prep supplied directly, since simplification happens building prep first place.

title: shapeindices: Shape Indices for (Multi)Polygons — shapeindices-package
text: Convexity, moment--inertia compactness, span (mean-pairwise-distance), radial concentration (mean-distance--geometric-median) indices (multi)polygons via constrained Delaunay triangulation (CDT), plus classic boundary/hull/bounding-box compactness scores redistricting literature (convex-hull ratio, Polsby-Popper, width-length ratio, Reock) need triangulation . See vignette("b-understanding-convexity-index", package = "shapeindices") full derivation worked examples.

title: shapeindices: Shape Indices for (Multi)Polygons — shapeindices-package
text: Maintainer: Nikhil Kaza kaza@cs.unc.edu Authors: Nikhil Kaza kaza@cs.unc.edu

title: Span index: mean pairwise interior distance vs. an equal-area circle — span_index
text: poly single sfg/sfc (MULTI)POLYGON deterministic TRUE (default), compute polygon's fixed CDT quadrature mesh - O(n^2). FALSE, Monte Carlo n_lines random interior point pairs instead - O(n_lines), useful mesh large. n_quad unused (error passed explicitly) deterministic = FALSE. n_quad quadrature points per triangle deterministic = TRUE (1 3), used cross-triangle term (-triangle term always computed separately, subdividing triangle). 3 (default) accurate; drop 1 meshes hundred triangles matters. n_lines number random point pairs sample deterministic = FALSE (default 3000), function(n_tri) - argument name convexity_index()'s Monte Carlo mode, one value passed shape_indices() sets every mesh index's sample count , though line geometry actually built . Warns substantially lower deterministic = TRUE need polygon. seed optional RNG seed, used deterministic = FALSE. prep optional pre-computed list(poly, tri) prepare_polygon(), skip re-triangulating also calling indices weight optional numeric vector, one entry per triangle prep$tri, substituting triangle's area/mass throughout. NULL (default) compares circle; otherwise reference becomes concentric annulus matching weight's histogram. points optional pre-drawn (2*n_lines)x2 coordinate matrix, meaningful deterministic = FALSE - primarily shape_indices()'s internal point-sharing mechanism (draw one sample reuse across convexity/span/radial_concentration instead drawing independently), typically supplied directly. simplify_tolerance passed prepare_polygon() prep NULL - see doc. Ignored (error) prep supplied directly, since simplification happens building prep first place.
